A ring buffer offers superior performance and cache locality for telemetry ingestion due to its contiguous memory layout and predictable access patterns, making it ideal for high-throughput, low-latency scenarios where the maximum data volume is known in advance. Since it operates on a fixed-size array with head and tail pointers, it avoids the overhead of dynamic memory allocation and pointer chasing inherent in linked structures, ensuring consistent write speeds even under heavy load. However, this approach requires pre-allocating the entire buffer
